ranked_movies.title: The Thing
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a masterpiece of practical effects and body horror. Praised for its tension, atmosphere, and the iconic defibrillator scene. Consistently appears in 'best of' lists.

ranked_movies.title: Hereditary
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned multiple times for its Oscar-worthy performances, particularly Toni Collette and Alex Wolff. Recognized as a modern classic that is deeply disturbing and impactful.

ranked_movies.title: Alien
ranked_movies.reason: Hailed as a cornerstone of sci-fi horror with one of the best monster designs ever. Frequently appears in top 5 and 'must-see' lists for horror fans.

ranked_movies.title: The Shining
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for its performances, particularly Shelley Duvall, and its iconic status. Regularly listed as a favorite and a foundational horror film.

ranked_movies.title: The Exorcist
ranked_movies.reason: A consistent entry in 'greatest horror' lists. Recognized for its cultural impact and enduring power to terrify.

ranked_movies.title: Scream
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended for its self-aware, fun nature and knowledge of horror tropes. Mentioned as a favorite franchise and a safe choice for the hypothetical survival scenario.

ranked_movies.title: Jaws
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed for Robert Shaw's Oscar-worthy performance and its profound cultural impact, even changing viewers' behavior around water. A perennial favorite.

ranked_movies.title: The Ring
ranked_movies.reason: Heavily debated regarding its villain's motives and frequently chosen as a theoretically survivable horror movie in the hypothetical scenario due to its 7-day rule.

ranked_movies.title: Candyman
ranked_movies.reason: Mentioned for Tony Todd's iconic performance and as a film where the villain has a sympathetic, valid grievance. A classic that is frequently recommended.

ranked_movies.title: Carrie
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently cited as a top example of a horror movie where the villain has a valid grievance. Recognized for its tragic and impactful story.

ranked_movies.title: Misery
ranked_movies.reason: Praised for Kathy Bates's Oscar-winning performance. Listed as a favorite and a must-see horror film.

ranked_movies.title: Event Horizon
ranked_movies.reason: Recommended as a must-see horror film, with its recovered footage scene cited as one of the craziest and most memorable.

ranked_movies.title: REC
ranked_movies.reason: Highly recommended as a foundational found-footage film. Its stairwell scene is specifically praised as a masterclass in tension and chaos.

ranked_movies.title: The Autopsy of Jane Doe
ranked_movies.reason: Frequently recommended as an underrated gem and a top-tier horror movie that is effective and well-crafted.

ranked_movies.title: The Fly
ranked_movies.reason: Jeff Goldblum's performance is hailed as Oscar-worthy. Celebrated as a brilliant body horror film and a must-watch.

ranked_movies.title: The Conjuring
ranked_movies.reason: Appears in multiple favorite lists. The recent discussion was sparked by a post about it, keeping it top-of-mind.

ranked_movies.title: Terrifier
ranked_movies.reason: Gained significant notoriety and discussion due to multiple posts about parents inappropriately bringing young children to see its extreme violence, particularly Terrifier 3.

ranked_movies.title: Midsommar
ranked_movies.reason: Chosen as a theoretically survivable horror movie and mentioned for its unique impact, even inspiring changes in viewers' lifestyles towards spirituality.

ranked_movies.title: Hellraiser
ranked_movies.reason: Discussed in the context of its iconic puzzle box (Lament Configuration) and listed as a favorite classic for its creativity and creepy atmosphere.

ranked_movies.title: The Substance
ranked_movies.reason: A recent film praised for Demi Moore's performance and its wild, impactful final act. Frequently mentioned alongside other modern body horror greats.
